$comment: opus-mind prompt registry. Lists files that ARE prompts (audit targets), not files that talk ABOUT prompts. Docs in README, primitives/, techniques/, annotations/, methodology/, BUILD.md are NOT prompts — do not include them.

prompts.note: skeleton + instructional prose around the prompt body. Scored on the 11-invariant scale as of v0.1.0. Current live score: 11/11.
intentional demo of a real prompt — shipped as a 'before' state for teaching. Floor at 8/11. Current live score: 10/11.

$historical: skills/opus-mind/SKILL.md was listed here with expected_score 6 on the old 6-invariant scale. It is instruction prose for Claude Code, not a production system prompt — auditing it with audit.py is a genre mismatch (see CHANGELOG v0.1.0). The informational --self CI step still logs its score for observability, but the registry gate no longer enforces it.

calibration_files.note: Canonical reverse-engineering source. The primary 6-invariant score MUST be 6/6 here — it is the floor that defines our thresholds. If this regresses, either (a) our regex broke, (b) CL4R1T4S drifted, or (c) we over-generalized a primitive. All three cases require human review. Stylebook mode (--stylebook) intentionally fails this file: the author's anti-slop list is not grounded in the source.
